title: herdr-orchestrating SKILL.md: the dispatch role, readable cold, with a dry-run contract
action: Author the document a cold agent reads at the start of every iteration. It must be self-sufficient: the agent remembers nothing between iterations, so every fact it needs is here or read live. Dispatch role: (1) self-name first via `herdr pane current --current` then `herdr pane rename` (D17); (2) refuse everything and report when gate_bypass is below full (D6); (3) count occupied slots from labelled runtime panes cross-checked against bee state, never from agent_status (D18, D20), cap 4 (D5); (4) build the dispatchable set per D1 - a reverse index over docs/history/*/CONTEXT.md keyed by each file's `**Backlog:**` line, intersected with in-flight rows, minus slugs that already have a worktree grant or any cell; (5) run cell 6's classify-lane.mjs on each candidate and drop every non-dispatchable one (D6); (6) rank the survivors by impact and announce the choice with its reason into the chat pane before acting (D16); (7) `bee worktree new --feature <slug>` from the MAIN checkout, then `herdr pane split --cwd <worktree>` on the runtime tab, then `herdr agent start` with argv carrying `--model sonnet --permission-mode bypassPermissions` (D4, D9, D22). Also state the anomaly rule: an unlabelled runtime pane, or a labelled one whose worktree has claimed cells but a dead session, is reported ONCE per anomaly - not every 60 seconds - and never silently reclaimed (D18). Chat pane discovery: the chat pane is the cockpit tab's leftmost pane, resolved via `herdr pane layout`; say so explicitly, because nothing labels the human's pane. Define --dry-run: report the whole decision, change nothing. Directory name is herdr-orchestrating (D21). Byte-identical copies in both roots.

truths: The skill cites D1, D5, D6, D14, D16, D17, D18, D20 and D22 by id
Occupancy comes from bee state and labels, never from agent_status as proof of completion
Every candidate passes through classify-lane.mjs before it can be chosen
The chat pane is discovered by a stated rule, not assumed to be labelled
An anomaly is reported once, not once per poll
The working agent is started with --model sonnet and --permission-mode bypassPermissions
--dry-run changes nothing and creates no pane or worktree

artifacts.substantive: dispatch-role instructions sufficient for a cold agent, with a dry-run contract

prohibitions: Do not instruct the agent to read herdr agent_status as proof an item is finished (D20)
Do not introduce a state file or registry (D18)
Do not include merge, cleanup, or pane-closing instructions; that is slice 2

verify: set -e; A=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/SKILL.md; B=.agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/SKILL.md; diff -q "$A" "$B"; for d in D1 D5 D6 D14 D16 D17 D18 D20 D22; do grep -qE "\b$d\b" "$A" || { echo "missing $d"; exit 1; }; done; grep -q 'classify-lane' "$A"; grep -q -- '--dry-run' "$A"; grep -q 'pane layout' "$A"; grep -q 'bypassPermissions' "$A"; ! grep -qiE 'cleanup|worktree merge' "$A"; echo SKILL_OK
